gate_type: V8 backtracking budget (Irregexp) limits regex execution — not exploitable on Node 16+
reasoning: The code pattern is confirmed real: three autoImport functions (opensshFormat, pkcs1Format, pkcs8Format) contain the nested-quantifier regex `(([A-Za-z0-9+/=]+\s*)+)` inside a lookahead+backreference, with an unsanitized call chain from importKey() / new NodeRSA(string) to all three handlers when no format is specified. However, a prior empirical verification on Node 22 / V8 12.4 (observation 1349) established that the vulnerability is not exploitable on any currently supported runtime: V8's Irregexp engine enforces a backtracking budget of approximately 1 million operations (introduced in V8 8.8, shipping with Node 16). When that budget is exhausted the engine returns a non-match immediately rather than continuing. Empirical testing with a 500,000-character adversarial input completed in under 25ms; 1,000 iterations of a 200-character adversarial input completed in under 1ms. Because Node 16 is the oldest supported LTS and the budget mitigation is unconditional (no opt-out flag), there is no default or non-default configuration that restores exploitability on a supported runtime. The gate is therefore not breachable, and severity is downgraded from high to low.
